description:
You are a machine–learning engineer who has just finished collecting raw CSV files under 
    /home/user/data/raw/.  
    Before you can start any experiments, the orchestration system you use (written in Go) 
    requires two small, human-readable configuration files—one in YAML and one in TOML—plus a very 
    terse log file confirming that you actually created them.

    What you must accomplish
    ------------------------
    1. Create the directory  /home/user/project/config/   (create any missing parent directories).
    2. Inside that directory, create a YAML file called  data_sources.yaml  whose exact, complete 
       contents must be **identical** to the block shown below (the indentation and the newline at 
       the end of the file are both significant and will be checked by an automated test):

       ```
       datasets:
         - name: cats_vs_dogs
           path: /home/user/data/raw/cats_vs_dogs.csv
           format: csv
           target_column: label
         - name: flowers102
           path: /home/user/data/raw/flowers102.csv
           format: csv
           target_column: category
         - name: cifar10
           path: /home/user/data/raw/cifar10.csv
           format: csv
           target_column: label
       ```

       Notes:
       • The file must end with exactly one trailing newline  
       • Use spaces—not tabs—for indentation (2 spaces per level)

    3. In the same  /home/user/project/config/  directory, create a TOML file called  
       training_config.toml  whose exact, complete contents must be **identical** to the block 
       shown below (again, the newline at the end of the file is significant):

       ```
       [training]
       batch_size = 64
       num_epochs = 30
       learning_rate = 0.001
       shuffle = true

       [data]
       augmentation = true
       cache_dir = "/home/user/data/cache"

       [output]
       model_dir = "/home/user/models"
       log_level = "INFO"
       ```

       Notes:
       • Keep the blank line preserved between each TOML table section  
       • File must end with exactly one trailing newline

    4. Create the directory  /home/user/project/logs/  (if not already present).

    5. Inside that logs directory, create a plaintext log file named  config_edit.log  that has 
       three lines—one for each file-creation action—using the semicolon-separated format:

           <filename>;<action>;OK

       Concretely, the file must end up containing **exactly** the following three lines (including 
       the final newline after the last line):

       ```
       data_sources.yaml;created;OK
       training_config.toml;created;OK
       config_edit.log;created;OK
       ```

       (Yes, the log file reports its own creation as the third line.)

    6. Verify that the two configuration files are valid by running at least one command that 
       parses each file without error (e.g., using yq for YAML and a Python one-liner with tomllib 
       for TOML, or any other command-line utilities available in the base image).  
       The parsing step is mainly for your benefit; only the presence and the exact byte-for-byte 
       contents of the three files will be automatically graded.

    Complexity budget
    -----------------
    A human would need roughly 5–10 terminal commands to:
    • create two directories,  
    • write two configuration files,  
    • append three short lines to a log, and  
    • optionally run one-liner validation commands.

    Deliverables summary
    --------------------
    After you are done, the judge will look for:

    1. /home/user/project/config/data_sources.yaml   (content must match the YAML block above)
    2. /home/user/project/config/training_config.toml   (content must match the TOML block above)
    3. /home/user/project/logs/config_edit.log   (three lines, exactly as specified)

    Make sure the paths, file names, exact text, indentation, blank lines, and trailing newlines 
    match the specification—automated byte-level comparison will be used.
truth:
<!-- Ground-truth reference for the automated grader.  DO NOT reveal in public description. -->
    <files>
        <file path="/home/user/project/config/data_sources.yaml"><![CDATA[
datasets:
  - name: cats_vs_dogs
    path: /home/user/data/raw/cats_vs_dogs.csv
    format: csv
    target_column: label
  - name: flowers102
    path: /home/user/data/raw/flowers102.csv
    format: csv
    target_column: category
  - name: cifar10
    path: /home/user/data/raw/cifar10.csv
    format: csv
    target_column: label
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/project/config/training_config.toml"><![CDATA[
[training]
batch_size = 64
num_epochs = 30
learning_rate = 0.001
shuffle = true

[data]
augmentation = true
cache_dir = "/home/user/data/cache"

[output]
model_dir = "/home/user/models"
log_level = "INFO"
]]></file>

        <file path="/home/user/project/logs/config_edit.log"><![CDATA[
data_sources.yaml;created;OK
training_config.toml;created;OK
config_edit.log;created;OK
]]></file>
    </files>
